title: Che Shares Two New Singles & SoundCloud-Only EP Fully Loaded
author: Tom Breihan

The post Che Shares Two New Singles & SoundCloud-Only EP <em>Fully Loaded</em> appeared first on Stereogum.
full_text:
Last year, the 19-year-old Atlanta rapper Che released his chaotic, overwhelming Rest In Bass, and he made his way onto our list of the year's best albums. At the very end of the year, he dropped a Rest In Bass deluxe edition with 14 extra songs. It you're into the guttural, instinctive sound of post-Playboi Carti rage rap, Che is apparently your guy, and you will be happy to learn that he's got a bunch of new music out today. 

Che has the great honor of headlining the upcoming Monster Energy Outbreak Tour, which kicks off next week in Santa Ana, and that's presumably why he just dropped two blown-out, unhinged new singles, "Million Dollar Mansion" and "Promoting Violence." Both of those songs also appear on Fully Loaded, a new SoundCloud-only EP that Che released today. "Million Dollar Mansion" and "Promoting Violence" are on all the regular streaming services, but you'll need to head to SoundCloud to hear the other three tracks, including "White Folk," the one where Che bellows about snorting blow with them white hoes.

After listening to all of this stuff, my brain is thoroughly scrambled. If you want to scramble yours, you can hear all those tracks below.
